sentences.en: These ranged from mainframe-centric systems where all of the users of a single computer could write messages to each other, to PC-centric systems where workstations would mount a network share to store and retrieve messages.
sentences.zh: 这些范围从以大型机为中心的系统（其中一台计算机的所有用户都可以互相写入消息）到以 PC 为中心的系统（其中工作站将安装网络共享来存储和检索消息）。

sentences.en: Pretty much any scheme you can come up with for moving messages was probably in use somewhere from roughly the 1960s to the 1990s, by which time the ARPANET-derived family of email implementations had taken hold.
sentences.zh: 几乎所有你能想出的用于移动消息的方案都可能在大约 20 世纪 60 年代到 90 年代的某个时间被使用，到那时，源自 ARPANET 的电子邮件实现系列已经占据主导地位。

blocks.html_en: This form of email has a clearer heritage, to Ray Tomlinson, who came up with the core idea that addresses could identify both a user and a host, and that some kind of open protocol could be used to send messages to another host when necessary. Over time, and with many revisions, Tomlinson's design became SMTP and was joined by protocols like IMAP that built out the form of email we use today. This is a form of email that is in some ways decentralized (any user is free to choose a host) and in other ways centralized (each host assumed to be continuously online to store-and-forward messages for its users). Tomlinson's design was flexible enough that we have not had to totally get rid of it, but enough has changed about the modern Internet that we have had to take a new approach.

sentences.en: This form of email has a clearer heritage, to Ray Tomlinson, who came up with the core idea that addresses could identify both a user and a host, and that some kind of open protocol could be used to send messages to another host when necessary.
sentences.zh: 对于 Ray Tomlinson 来说，这种形式的电子邮件具有更清晰的传统，他提出了地址可以识别用户和主机的核心思想，并且可以在必要时使用某种开放协议将消息发送到另一个主机。

sentences.en: Over time, and with many revisions, Tomlinson's design became SMTP and was joined by protocols like IMAP that built out the form of email we use today.
sentences.zh: 随着时间的推移，经过多次修改，Tomlinson 的设计变成了 SMTP，并加入了 IMAP 等协议，构建了我们今天使用的电子邮件形式。

sentences.en: This is a form of email that is in some ways decentralized (any user is free to choose a host) and in other ways centralized (each host assumed to be continuously online to store-and-forward messages for its users).
sentences.zh: 这是一种电子邮件形式，在某些方面是分散的（任何用户都可以自由选择主机），在其他方面是集中的（假设每个主机持续在线为其用户存储和转发消息）。

sentences.en: Tomlinson's design was flexible enough that we have not had to totally get rid of it, but enough has changed about the modern Internet that we have had to take a new approach.
sentences.zh: 汤姆林森的设计足够灵活，我们不必完全摆脱它，但现代互联网已经发生了足够的变化，我们不得不采取新的方法。

blocks.html_en: Email has many vexing limitations, artifacts of its age. For example, email handling should not be assumed to be "8-bit clean"—email protocols were originally defined over 7-bit ASCII and ran on many machines that used the eighth bit as a checksum. These machines were prone to changing the last bit of each byte, or otherwise mishandling email with 8-bit content. That wasn't a problem when text was completely limited to that 7-bit plane, but both Unicode and the desire to send binary files made 7-bit email unworkable. MIME was developed as a workaround, an encoding technique that solves a few problems in one go by encoding all non-ASCII content of email in the form of ASCII characters.

sentences.en: Email has many vexing limitations, artifacts of its age.
sentences.zh: 电子邮件有许多令人烦恼的限制，这是它那个时代的产物。

sentences.en: For example, email handling should not be assumed to be "8-bit clean"—email protocols were originally defined over 7-bit ASCII and ran on many machines that used the eighth bit as a checksum.
sentences.zh: 例如，电子邮件处理不应被假定为“8 位干净”——电子邮件协议最初是通过 7 位 ASCII 定义的，并在许多使用第八位作为校验和的机器上运行。

sentences.en: These machines were prone to changing the last bit of each byte, or otherwise mishandling email with 8-bit content.
sentences.zh: 这些机器很容易更改每个字节的最后一位，或者错误地处理 8 位内容的电子邮件。

sentences.en: That wasn't a problem when text was completely limited to that 7-bit plane, but both Unicode and the desire to send binary files made 7-bit email unworkable.
sentences.zh: 当文本完全限于 7 位平面时，这不是问题，但 Unicode 和发送二进制文件的愿望使得 7 位电子邮件无法使用。

sentences.en: MIME was developed as a workaround, an encoding technique that solves a few problems in one go by encoding all non-ASCII content of email in the form of ASCII characters.
sentences.zh: MIME 是作为一种解决方法而开发的，它是一种编码技术，通过将电子邮件的所有非 ASCII 内容编码为 ASCII 字符的形式，一次性解决了一些问题。

blocks.html_en: This is rather inelegant. MIME's ASCII encoding, whether quoted-printable style or base64 style, is annoying for humans to read and inefficient with regard to message size. As with all base64-like encodings, it counts as a suspicious smell, an indication that we are papering over past mistakes. Email is full of them. Let's consider another: security.

sentences.en: This is rather inelegant.
sentences.zh: 这是相当不优雅的。

sentences.en: MIME's ASCII encoding, whether quoted-printable style or base64 style, is annoying for humans to read and inefficient with regard to message size.
sentences.zh: MIME 的 ASCII 编码，无论是引用打印样式还是 base64 样式，对于人类阅读来说都很烦人，并且在消息大小方面效率低下。

sentences.en: As with all base64-like encodings, it counts as a suspicious smell, an indication that we are papering over past mistakes.
sentences.zh: 与所有类似 Base64 的编码一样，它被视为一种可疑的气味，表明我们正在掩盖过去的错误。

sentences.en: Email is full of them.
sentences.zh: 电子邮件中充满了它们。

sentences.en: Let's consider another: security.
sentences.zh: 让我们考虑另一个问题：安全性。

blocks.html_en: "Internet email," a term we can use to try to explicitly specify the SMTP-type email system inherited from ARPANET, uses a simple design that dates to an era when the operators of networked computers all knew each other. While there were other possibilities, most email was delivered directly to the computer of the recipient, and in a lot of cases the host-level security of the era wasn't very good either. The possibility that someone else could read your private email was known and there wasn't a whole lot you could do about it. The internet existed within a context of trust.

sentences.en: "Internet email," a term we can use to try to explicitly specify the SMTP-type email system inherited from ARPANET, uses a simple design that dates to an era when the operators of networked computers all knew each other.
sentences.zh: “互联网电子邮件”是一个术语，我们可以用来尝试明确指定从 ARPANET 继承的 SMTP 类型电子邮件系统，它使用简单的设计，可以追溯到网络计算机操作员都互相认识的时代。

sentences.en: While there were other possibilities, most email was delivered directly to the computer of the recipient, and in a lot of cases the host-level security of the era wasn't very good either.
sentences.zh: 虽然还有其他可能性，但大多数电子邮件都是直接发送到收件人的计算机，并且在很多情况下，那个时代的主机级安全性也不是很好。

sentences.en: The possibility that someone else could read your private email was known and there wasn't a whole lot you could do about it.
sentences.zh: 其他人可能会阅读您的私人电子邮件，这是众所周知的，但您对此无能为力。

sentences.en: The internet existed within a context of trust.
sentences.zh: 互联网存在于信任的背景下。

blocks.html_en: We don't extend that same trust today. Email is usually handled by third-party mail services on either end, which are not necessarily trustworthy, and to get between the two third-party mail services it has to pass through a series of internet links that are also of questionable security. Email became one of the first, probably the first, compelling application of large-scale computer networking. It may have also been the first of the field's enduring security failures. The system was designed with no concern for message confidentiality or integrity, and much of it still operates that way today.

sentences.en: We don't extend that same trust today.
sentences.zh: 今天我们不再给予同样的信任。

sentences.en: Email is usually handled by third-party mail services on either end, which are not necessarily trustworthy, and to get between the two third-party mail services it has to pass through a series of internet links that are also of questionable security.
sentences.zh: 电子邮件通常由两端的第三方邮件服务处理，这些服务不一定值得信赖，并且为了在两个第三方邮件服务之间进行通信，必须通过一系列安全性也存在问题的互联网链接。

sentences.en: Email became one of the first, probably the first, compelling application of large-scale computer networking.
sentences.zh: 电子邮件成为大规模计算机网络最早、可能是第一个引人注目的应用之一。

sentences.en: It may have also been the first of the field's enduring security failures.
sentences.zh: 这也可能是该领域持久的安全失败中的第​​一个。

sentences.en: The system was designed with no concern for message confidentiality or integrity, and much of it still operates that way today.
sentences.zh: 该系统的设计并未考虑消息的机密性或完整性，并且大部分系统至今仍以这种方式运行。

blocks.html_en: Still, there's a long history of efforts to improve email security, and we should probably start the story with the greatest of all: not just an email protocol, but an entire stack of protocols that promised a better internet in general: the OSI suite.

sentences.en: Still, there's a long history of efforts to improve email security, and we should probably start the story with the greatest of all: not just an email protocol, but an entire stack of protocols that promised a better internet in general: the OSI suite.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，提高电子邮件安全性的努力已有很长的历史，我们可能应该从最伟大的开始：不仅仅是电子邮件协议，而是承诺更好的互联网的整个协议堆栈：OSI 套件。

blocks.html_en: The modern internet was born of an era often called the "protocol wars." When we tell the story of the internet today, we tend to simplify it to a nice linear tale in which ARPANET was created, everyone thought it was a good idea, and it proliferated. That's not totally wrong, but it leaves out a lot of the other actors. The telecom industry famously had its objections to the packet-switched model, which had originated from the computer industry rather than the communications industry and showed it. During the 1970s and 1980s, the telecom industry defined its own set of network protocols, and fundamental approach to computer networking, which came to be known as the OSI protocol suite.

sentences.en: The modern internet was born of an era often called the "protocol wars."
sentences.zh: 现代互联网诞生于一个通常被称为“协议战争”的时代。

sentences.en: When we tell the story of the internet today, we tend to simplify it to a nice linear tale in which ARPANET was created, everyone thought it was a good idea, and it proliferated.
sentences.zh: 当我们今天讲述互联网的故事时，我们倾向于将其简化为一个很好的线性故事，其中阿帕网被创建，每个人都认为这是一个好主意，并且它激增。

sentences.en: That's not totally wrong, but it leaves out a lot of the other actors.
sentences.zh: 这并不完全错误，但它遗漏了很多其他演员。

sentences.en: The telecom industry famously had its objections to the packet-switched model, which had originated from the computer industry rather than the communications industry and showed it.
sentences.zh: 电信行业对分组交换模型的反对是众所周知的，分组交换模型起源于计算机行业而不是通信行业，并表明了这一点。

sentences.en: During the 1970s and 1980s, the telecom industry defined its own set of network protocols, and fundamental approach to computer networking, which came to be known as the OSI protocol suite.
sentences.zh: 在 20 世纪 70 年代和 80 年代，电信行业定义了自己的一套网络协议和计算机网络的基本方法，后来被称为 OSI 协议套件。

blocks.html_en: Today, the relevance of the OSI protocols is limited to the frustrating insistence of modern networking textbooks on trying to explain the internet in terms of the "OSI model," a now more speculative than actual layer model that describes a different system incompatible with the internet. The reason that academia has this weird fixation on using the design documentation of a failed internet to explain the fundamentally different architecture of the other internet that succeeded... well, that itself is fallout of the protocol wars. History may be written by the victors, but the losers still get to dictate theory.

sentences.en: Today, the relevance of the OSI protocols is limited to the frustrating insistence of modern networking textbooks on trying to explain the internet in terms of the "OSI model," a now more speculative than actual layer model that describes a different system incompatible with the internet.
sentences.zh: 今天，OSI 协议的相关性仅限于现代网络教科书令人沮丧地坚持试图用“OSI 模型”来解释互联网，这种模型现在比实际的层模型更具推测性，它描述了与互联网不兼容的不同系统。

sentences.en: The reason that academia has this weird fixation on using the design documentation of a failed internet to explain the fundamentally different architecture of the other internet that succeeded...
sentences.zh: 学术界之所以如此奇怪地执着于使用失败的互联网的设计文档来解释另一个成功的互联网的根本不同的架构......

sentences.en: well, that itself is fallout of the protocol wars.
sentences.zh: 好吧，这本身就是协议战争的后果。

sentences.en: History may be written by the victors, but the losers still get to dictate theory.
sentences.zh: 历史可能是由胜利者书写的，但失败者仍然可以主宰理论。

blocks.html_en: Despite my ongoing project to discourage the use of the OSI model for learning, we can indeed learn from the OSI protocols. The reasons that the TCP/IP stack succeeded over the OSI stack are many, one of them being that OSI's "design by committee" approach led to a set of protocols that are very thoroughly thought out but quite complex. This is a big difference from the internet protocols, whose design philosophy was closer to "good enough for now."

sentences.en: Despite my ongoing project to discourage the use of the OSI model for learning, we can indeed learn from the OSI protocols.
sentences.zh: 尽管我正在进行的项目不鼓励使用 OSI 模型进行学习，但我们确实可以从 OSI 协议中学习。

sentences.en: The reasons that the TCP/IP stack succeeded over the OSI stack are many, one of them being that OSI's "design by committee" approach led to a set of protocols that are very thoroughly thought out but quite complex.
sentences.zh: TCP/IP 堆栈比 OSI 堆栈成功的原因有很多，其中之一是 OSI 的“委员会设计”方法导致了一组经过深思熟虑但相当复杂的协议。

sentences.en: This is a big difference from the internet protocols, whose design philosophy was closer to "good enough for now."
sentences.zh: 这与互联网协议有很大的不同，互联网协议的设计理念更接近于“目前已经足够好了”。

blocks.html_en: The difference in philosophy is especially stark when we look at the application-layer protocols. The "internet protocol suite" as it is conventionally defined does not include application protocols at all, itself a telling fact. The OSI suite does. Internet email started out as a special-case use of FTP, before moving over to a loose set of commands over Telnet that eventually formalized (or ossified, depending on who you ask) into SMTP.

sentences.en: The difference in philosophy is especially stark when we look at the application-layer protocols.
sentences.zh: 当我们研究应用层协议时，理念上的差异尤其明显。

sentences.en: The "internet protocol suite" as it is conventionally defined does not include application protocols at all, itself a telling fact.
sentences.zh: 传统定义的“互联网协议套件”根本不包括应用程序协议，这本身就是一个有说服力的事实。

sentences.en: The OSI suite does.
sentences.zh: OSI 套件可以。

sentences.en: Internet email started out as a special-case use of FTP, before moving over to a loose set of commands over Telnet that eventually formalized (or ossified, depending on who you ask) into SMTP.
sentences.zh: 互联网电子邮件最初是 FTP 的特殊用途，后来转向 Telnet 上的一组松散命令，最终形式化（或僵化，取决于你问的是谁）为 SMTP。

blocks.html_en: By the time the OSI standards were published in 1984, it was known that email was one of the killer applications—the first revision of SMTP was published in 1982, a post-facto response to the popularity of email on the internet, and besides, email had been a key feature of most proprietary networking systems since the 1970s. So, OSI had a solution: X.400.

sentences.en: By the time the OSI standards were published in 1984, it was known that email was one of the killer applications—the first revision of SMTP was published in 1982, a post-facto response to the popularity of email on the internet, and besides, email had been a key feature of most proprietary networking systems since the 1970s.
sentences.zh: 当 OSI 标准于 1984 年发布时，人们已经知道电子邮件是杀手级应用之一——SMTP 的第一个修订版于 1982 年发布，这是对电子邮件在互联网上流行的事后回应，此外，自 20 世纪 70 年代以来，电子邮件一直是大多数专有网络系统的关键功能。

sentences.en: So, OSI had a solution: X.400.
sentences.zh: 因此，OSI 提出了一个解决方案：X.400。

blocks.html_en: X.400 is the "Message Handling System" part of the OSI suite, and provides features which are recognizable as email but also a bit more generalized. X.400 was, in most regards, a failure. It was unsuccessful in displacing internet email in almost all environments. Still, X.400 was decidedly influential. There was a period, albeit brief, in which it was thought that major governments would require X.400 compatibility in future purchasing. This was similar, and had a similar effect, to the period where POSIX-compatibility was a government requirement: a lot of vendors designed their products to meet the requirement, the government lost interest, and it became a curious legacy. In email, this is most obvious in the form of Microsoft Exchange: Exchange started out as an X.400 implementation, mostly for government reasons, and still provides a lot of X.400 features.

sentences.en: X.400 is the "Message Handling System" part of the OSI suite, and provides features which are recognizable as email but also a bit more generalized.
sentences.zh: X.400 是 OSI 套件的“消息处理系统”部分，提供可识别为电子邮件但也更通用的功能。

sentences.en: X.400 was, in most regards, a failure.
sentences.zh: 从大多数方面来说，X.400 都是失败的。

sentences.en: It was unsuccessful in displacing internet email in almost all environments.
sentences.zh: 它未能成功地在几乎所有环境中取代互联网电子邮件。

sentences.en: Still, X.400 was decidedly influential.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，X.400 仍然具有绝对的影响力。

sentences.en: There was a period, albeit brief, in which it was thought that major governments would require X.400 compatibility in future purchasing.
sentences.zh: 曾经有一段时间，尽管时间很短，但人们认为主要政府在未来的采购中会要求兼容 X.400。

sentences.en: This was similar, and had a similar effect, to the period where POSIX-compatibility was a government requirement: a lot of vendors designed their products to meet the requirement, the government lost interest, and it became a curious legacy.
sentences.zh: 这与 POSIX 兼容性成为政府要求的时期相似，并且具有相似的效果：许多供应商设计他们的产品来满足要求，政府失去了兴趣，它成为一个奇怪的遗产。

sentences.en: In email, this is most obvious in the form of Microsoft Exchange: Exchange started out as an X.400 implementation, mostly for government reasons, and still provides a lot of X.400 features.
sentences.zh: 在电子邮件中，这以 Microsoft Exchange 的形式最为明显：Exchange 最初是作为 X.400 实现（主要是出于政府原因），并且仍然提供许多 X.400 功能。

blocks.html_en: X.400 is vastly more complex than internet email. For example, the encoding of X.400 messages is ASN.1, a binary serialization format that is more efficient and capable than MIME but also a lot more complicated. That X.400 has complexity stacked on top of complexity is useful in understanding why one of X.400's most compelling features didn't go very far: encryption.

sentences.en: X.400 is vastly more complex than internet email.
sentences.zh: X.400 比互联网电子邮件复杂得多。

sentences.en: For example, the encoding of X.400 messages is ASN.1, a binary serialization format that is more efficient and capable than MIME but also a lot more complicated.

sentences.en: That X.400 has complexity stacked on top of complexity is useful in understanding why one of X.400's most compelling features didn't go very far: encryption.
sentences.zh: X.400 的复杂性非常高，这有助于理解为什么 X.400 最引人注目的功能之一没有走得太远：加密。

blocks.html_en: If you are sending an email to someone, and you don't want anyone else to read it, the most obvious approach is end-to-end encryption. For many practical reasons, you'll want to use asymmetric encryption. You find a public key for the recipient, encrypt the message to that key, and then send it over the wire as usual. The recipient then decrypts it with their private key. Simple enough, except that every part of this process is laden with complexity.

sentences.zh: 如果您要向某人发送电子邮件，并且不希望其他人阅读它，最明显的方法是端到端加密。

sentences.zh: 然后接收者用他们的私钥解密它。

sentences.zh: 很简单，只是这个过程的每个部分都充满了复杂性。

blocks.html_en: To underscore that point, let's take a look at X.400 itself—the 1988 "Blue Book" version, which seems most appropriate to when email was ossifying.

Recommendations for other types of key management schemes, including symmetric encryption, to support the security features are for further study.

blocks.html_en: End-to-end encrypted email requires two basic parts:

sentences.en: End-to-end encrypted email requires two basic parts:
sentences.zh: 端到端加密电子邮件需要两个基本部分：

blocks.html_en: First, there must be a technical format for moving encrypted messages through the message transfer system. X.400 handily resolves this by the use of ASN.1 definitions that allow for encrypted messages bodies, thus making most of that someone else's problem. X.400 actually contemplated a lot more when it comes to security features, things like non-repudiation of delivery, that required more complexity in message transfer but didn't make it to the modern day. We'll just ignore those for now.

sentences.en: First, there must be a technical format for moving encrypted messages through the message transfer system.
sentences.zh: 首先，必须有一种通过消息传输系统传输加密消息的技术格式。

sentences.en: X.400 handily resolves this by the use of ASN.1 definitions that allow for encrypted messages bodies, thus making most of that someone else's problem.
sentences.zh: X.400 通过使用允许加密消息体的 ASN.1 定义轻松解决了这个问题，从而解决了大部分其他人的问题。

sentences.en: X.400 actually contemplated a lot more when it comes to security features, things like non-repudiation of delivery, that required more complexity in message transfer but didn't make it to the modern day.

sentences.en: We'll just ignore those for now.
sentences.zh: 我们暂时忽略这些。

blocks.html_en: Second, there has to be a key infrastructure. You need some way of obtaining a public key for the person you want to send a message, and of being sure that it really belongs to them. This is important both for encrypting outbound email and for authenticating inbound email, since end-to-end encryption is usually deployed alongside end-to-end authentication using the same cryptographic infrastructure. In the case of X.400, that whole problem is deferred to the X.500 family of protocols.

blocks.html_en: What is X.500? It's our old friend, directory access protocol. The X.500 directory standards are the comparison by which LDAP is "lightweight," and there is a parallel story: LDAP is the scrappy internet protocol that won against the X.500 Goliath.

blocks.html_en: To the extent that X.400 succeeded, its security features were actually a big motivation. The ISODE Consortium, which had created something like a reference implementation of much of the OSI application suite, is still around today as Isode Ltd and still sells a message handling system. There are a few other maintained X.400 descendants as well. One of the largest customer bases is military: while less common in the US (keep in mind that internet email was originally developed under the aegis of the US military), a number of European countries adopted X.400 as their secure messaging solution and still have extensive reliance on it in military and intelligence organizations. Other enduring strongholds of X.400 include global aviation (ICAO's messaging system is X.400-based) and electronic data interchange, or EDI, a standardized system of financial and supply chain messaging between ERPs.

blocks.html_en: It is no coincidence that these are the same kinds of applications where protocols like X.25 survived unusually late (indicating a high affinity to long-running, legacy systems) and that they are contexts where the participants and messaging are governed by a central entity. That means that a directory exists, which means that the key distribution problem is vastly simplified. It also means that the software in use, both transfer agents (e.g. servers) and clients, are standardized and usually purchased from vendors that have specifically designed them to work well in an X.400 environment.

This means that there is no meaningful directory capability in the email system. Email is also used with a hugely diverse landscape of clients, imposing a steep penalty on the "complicated message encoding that requires interaction with a complicated directory system" part of the design.

blocks.html_en: MIME

First, it's helpful to expand a bit on the involvement of MIME, the Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions. MIME originated as a solution to the 8-bit problem with internet email, as a way to encode non-ASCII text and binary files in a consistent, reliable way. Along the way to achieving this aim, it introduces the concept of a "multipart" message which has multiple distinct objects as a body instead of just one string of text. We use this multipart feature heavily today, both for obvious applications like file attachments and for more subtle ones like HTML emails that provide a plaintext variant. I don't think it's totally wrong to summarize MIME as "email 2.0," because while it is limited in scope to the message format (not changing the transport protocols), MIME adds a lot of new functionality to email that we now consider basic requirements for a messaging system.

sentences.en: I don't think it's totally wrong to summarize MIME as "email 2.0," because while it is limited in scope to the message format (not changing the transport protocols), MIME adds a lot of new functionality to email that we now consider basic requirements for a messaging system.

blocks.html_en: The history of MIME has a lot more twists and turns than you might expect, which reflects the difficulty of making major changes to a system as distributed and heterogeneous as internet email. Still, the MIME authors seem to have had a similar view that MIME was a significant evolution of email by standardizing message bodies and paving the way for future extensions to that standard, adding new functionality. blocks.html_en: STD 11, RFC 822 defines a message representation protocol which specifies considerable detail about message headers, but which leaves the message content, or message body, as flat ASCII text. This document redefines the format of message bodies to allow multi-part textual and non-textual message bodies to be represented and exchanged without loss of information. This is based on earlier work documented in RFC 934 and STD 11, RFC 1049, but extends and revises that work. Because RFC 822 said so little about message bodies, this document is largely orthogonal to (rather than a revision of) RFC 822.

sentences.zh: 这是基于 RFC 934 和 STD 11、RFC 1049 中记录的早期工作，但扩展和修订了该工作。

sentences.en: Because RFC 822 said so little about message bodies, this document is largely orthogonal to (rather than a revision of) RFC 822.
sentences.zh: 由于 RFC 822 对消息体的描述很少，因此本文档在很大程度上与 RFC 822 正交（而不是修订版）。

blocks.html_en: In other words, existing email standards addressed how messages are conveyed between hosts, but did little to address what would actually go into those messages. MIME addresses that problem by creating a proper standard for message bodies, one that adds features while contending with the unfortunate limitations of the many pre-MIME email implementations. The many RFCs around the general topic of MIME (and other proposals for email body formats) spend a lot of time contemplating how these new message types would be handled by email clients that did not understand them. Email technology is generally easy, email standards are hard.

sentences.en: In other words, existing email standards addressed how messages are conveyed between hosts, but did little to address what would actually go into those messages.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，现有的电子邮件标准解决了如何在主机之间传送消息，但几乎没有解决这些消息的实际内容。

sentences.en: MIME addresses that problem by creating a proper standard for message bodies, one that adds features while contending with the unfortunate limitations of the many pre-MIME email implementations.
sentences.zh: MIME 通过为消息正文创建适当的标准来解决该问题，该标准在增加功能的同时克服了许多 MIME 之前的电子邮件实现的不幸限制。

sentences.en: The many RFCs around the general topic of MIME (and other proposals for email body formats) spend a lot of time contemplating how these new message types would be handled by email clients that did not understand them.
sentences.zh: 许多围绕 MIME（以及电子邮件正文格式的其他提案）一般主题的 RFC 花费了大量时间思考不理解这些新消息类型的电子邮件客户端将如何处理它们。

sentences.en: Email technology is generally easy, email standards are hard.
sentences.zh: 电子邮件技术通常很容易，但电子邮件标准很难。

blocks.html_en: PGP

As these standards efforts marched on, the fast-growing field of computer cryptography had its own ideas. The first major event was cryptographer Phil Zimmerman's unceremonious 1991 release of a tool called PGP, or Pretty Good Privacy. The story of PGP is a long one, jumping from a casual release to an activist Usenet group to criminal charges to the foundation of PGP Inc to commercialize the system.

sentences.en: As these standards efforts marched on, the fast-growing field of computer cryptography had its own ideas.
sentences.zh: 随着这些标准工作的进展，快速发展的计算机密码学领域有了自己的想法。

sentences.en: The first major event was cryptographer Phil Zimmerman's unceremonious 1991 release of a tool called PGP, or Pretty Good Privacy.
sentences.zh: 第一个重大事件是密码学家 Phil Zimmerman 于 1991 年毫不客气地发布了一个名为 PGP（即 Pretty Good Privacy）的工具。

sentences.en: The story of PGP is a long one, jumping from a casual release to an activist Usenet group to criminal charges to the foundation of PGP Inc to commercialize the system.
sentences.zh: PGP 的故事很长，从偶然发布到活跃的 Usenet 团体，再到刑事指控，再到 PGP Inc 的成立以将该系统商业化。

blocks.html_en: The funny thing is that PGP itself is not that important to the story of PGP. Despite the corporate ambitions of the company bearing that name, PGP started as a loosely academic or activist project, and it ended up that way in the long term as the open-source clone GnuPG (GPG) displaced PGP in the marketplace. PGP Inc became part of Symantec and faded into the obscurity that most Symantec products do. Along the way, PGP and GPG implementations rode the roller coaster that was 1990s cryptography, swerving from algorithm to algorithm and growing a complex cipher selection system along the way.

sentences.en: The funny thing is that PGP itself is not that important to the story of PGP.
sentences.zh: 有趣的是，PGP 本身对于 PGP 的故事来说并不是那么重要。

sentences.en: Despite the corporate ambitions of the company bearing that name, PGP started as a loosely academic or activist project, and it ended up that way in the long term as the open-source clone GnuPG (GPG) displaced PGP in the marketplace.
sentences.zh: 尽管以该名称命名的公司有着企业雄心，但 PGP 最初是一个松散的学术或激进项目，从长远来看，随着开源克隆 GnuPG (GPG) 在市场上取代了 PGP，它最终以这种方式结束。

sentences.en: PGP Inc became part of Symantec and faded into the obscurity that most Symantec products do.
sentences.zh: PGP Inc 成为赛门铁克的一部分，并像大多数赛门铁克产品一样逐渐消失。

sentences.en: Along the way, PGP and GPG implementations rode the roller coaster that was 1990s cryptography, swerving from algorithm to algorithm and growing a complex cipher selection system along the way.
sentences.zh: 一路走来，PGP 和 GPG 的实现就像 20 世纪 90 年代的密码学一样坐上了过山车，从一个算法转向另一个算法，并一路发展出一个复杂的密码选择系统。

blocks.html_en: PGP was a general encryption tool and could be used for files or messages of any kind, but email was a key application. So how is a PGP-encrypted email actually conveyed? Well, it's not exactly pretty, but it aligns with the other messes we've already seen. PGP typically outputs ciphertext as either binary data or in a format called "ASCII-armored," which is base64 except with an optional CRC checksum trailer (which was deprecated decades ago). This is very similar to the PEM format widely used with modern cryptographic applications, and not by coincidence: PEM stands for Privacy-Enhanced Mail and is itself an artifact of a failed email encryption standard that produced quite a few RFCs. PGP's email application and PEM were under development just about simultaneously, and there was definitely cross-pollination of ideas between the two. PGP won, but not without taking parts of PEM along for the ride.

sentences.en: PGP was a general encryption tool and could be used for files or messages of any kind, but email was a key application.
sentences.zh: PGP 是一种通用加密工具，可用于任何类型的文件或消息，但电子邮件是一个关键应用。

sentences.en: So how is a PGP-encrypted email actually conveyed?
sentences.zh: 那么 PGP 加密的电子邮件实际上是如何传送的呢？

sentences.en: Well, it's not exactly pretty, but it aligns with the other messes we've already seen.
sentences.zh: 嗯，它并不完全漂亮，但它与我们已经看到的其他混乱情况一致。

sentences.en: PGP typically outputs ciphertext as either binary data or in a format called "ASCII-armored," which is base64 except with an optional CRC checksum trailer (which was deprecated decades ago).
sentences.zh: PGP 通常以二进制数据或称为“ASCII-armored”的格式输出密文，该格式为 base64，但带有可选的 CRC 校验和尾部（几十年前已弃用）。

sentences.en: This is very similar to the PEM format widely used with modern cryptographic applications, and not by coincidence: PEM stands for Privacy-Enhanced Mail and is itself an artifact of a failed email encryption standard that produced quite a few RFCs.
sentences.zh: 这与现代加密应用程序广泛使用的 PEM 格式非常相似，这并非巧合：PEM 代表隐私增强型邮件，它本身就是失败的电子邮件加密标准的产物，该标准产生了相当多的 RFC。

sentences.en: PGP's email application and PEM were under development just about simultaneously, and there was definitely cross-pollination of ideas between the two.
sentences.zh: PGP 的电子邮件应用程序和 PEM 几乎同时开发，两者之间肯定存在思想的交叉授粉。

sentences.en: PGP won, but not without taking parts of PEM along for the ride.
sentences.zh: PGP 获胜了，但也没有 PEM 的一部分参与其中。

blocks.html_en: In practice, ASCII-armored PGP payloads are sent one of two ways: first is "inline" format in which each part of a MIME-encoded email is separately encrypted and have otherwise usual MIME types (e.g. application/octet-stream), so that non-PGP-capable email clients will treat them as opaque files that the user can save off and process with a separate tool. The second approach is the "MIME" format in which the entire MIME-encoded email is encrypted whole and dropped into a new MIME document with the type "application/pgp-encrypted." The latter has better security properties (less metadata leakage) and tends to be more user-friendly and elegant in clients that are designed with PGP in mind, but fails very badly in non-PGP-capable clients which are not even able to separate out attachments. You can still process the email with an external PGP tool by saving off the whole body, MIME-decoding, decrypting, and MIME-decoding again, but you get a little frustrated just reading that, don't you?

sentences.en: In practice, ASCII-armored PGP payloads are sent one of two ways: first is "inline" format in which each part of a MIME-encoded email is separately encrypted and have otherwise usual MIME types (e.g. application/octet-stream), so that non-PGP-capable email clients will treat them as opaque files that the user can save off and process with a separate tool.
sentences.zh: 实际上，ASCII 防护的 PGP 有效负载以两种方式之一发送：第一种是“内联”格式，其中 MIME 编码的电子邮件的每个部分都单独加密，并具有其他常见的 MIME 类型（例如应用程序/八位字节流），以便不支持 PGP 的电子邮件客户端将它们视为不透明文件，用户可以保存并使用单独的工具进行处理。

sentences.en: The second approach is the "MIME" format in which the entire MIME-encoded email is encrypted whole and dropped into a new MIME document with the type "application/pgp-encrypted."
sentences.zh: 第二种方法是“MIME”格式，其中整个 MIME 编码的电子邮件被整体加密并放入类型为“application/pgp-encrypted”的新 MIME 文档中。

sentences.en: The latter has better security properties (less metadata leakage) and tends to be more user-friendly and elegant in clients that are designed with PGP in mind, but fails very badly in non-PGP-capable clients which are not even able to separate out attachments.
sentences.zh: 后者具有更好的安全属性（更少的元数据泄漏），并且在设计时考虑到 PGP 的客户端中往往更加用户友好和优雅，但在不支持 PGP 的客户端中会严重失败，甚至无法分离附件。

sentences.en: You can still process the email with an external PGP tool by saving off the whole body, MIME-decoding, decrypting, and MIME-decoding again, but you get a little frustrated just reading that, don't you?
sentences.zh: 您仍然可以使用外部 PGP 工具处理电子邮件，方法是保存整个正文、MIME 解码、解密和再次 MIME 解码，但您只是阅读这些内容就会感到有点沮丧，不是吗？

blocks.html_en: Today, this mess has become one of the two main criticisms of PGP. PGP is not the result of a huge, multi-party standards effort like X.400, but rather the product of a small group of people taking a pragmatic approach. Nonetheless, it grew up in the same climate and so survived with most of the same scars. From bottom to top, PGP is very complicated. Almost every part of PGP-encrypted email has multiple options, some of which are known to be insecure. Modern tools mostly follow sane defaults, but plenty of older tools remain in use. Key tools like gpg itself show their age with some of the least user-friendly interfaces ever designed. The immense difficulty faced by PGP users was the subject of seminal 1999 "usable security" paper "Why Johnny Can't Encrypt" (the paper that more or less invented the field of "usable security"), and most of the criticisms raised in that paper are still true of PGP implementations 27 years later.

sentences.en: Today, this mess has become one of the two main criticisms of PGP.
sentences.zh: 如今，这种混乱局面已成为 PGP 的两大诟病之一。

sentences.en: PGP is not the result of a huge, multi-party standards effort like X.400, but rather the product of a small group of people taking a pragmatic approach.
sentences.zh: PGP 并不是像 X.400 这样的大规模、多方标准努力的结果，而是一小群人采取务实方法的产物。

sentences.en: Nonetheless, it grew up in the same climate and so survived with most of the same scars.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，它还是在相同的气候下长大的，因此幸存下来，留下了大部分相同的伤疤。

sentences.en: From bottom to top, PGP is very complicated.
sentences.zh: 从下到上，PGP 非常复杂。

sentences.en: Almost every part of PGP-encrypted email has multiple options, some of which are known to be insecure.
sentences.zh: PGP 加密电子邮件的几乎每个部分都有多个选项，其中一些已知是不安全的。

sentences.en: Modern tools mostly follow sane defaults, but plenty of older tools remain in use.
sentences.zh: 现代工具大多遵循合理的默认设置，但许多旧工具仍在使用。

sentences.en: Key tools like gpg itself show their age with some of the least user-friendly interfaces ever designed.
sentences.zh: 像 gpg 这样的关键工具本身就显示出它们的年龄，其设计的界面是最不用户友好的。

sentences.en: The immense difficulty faced by PGP users was the subject of seminal 1999 "usable security" paper "Why Johnny Can't Encrypt" (the paper that more or less invented the field of "usable security"), and most of the criticisms raised in that paper are still true of PGP implementations 27 years later.
sentences.zh: PGP 用户面临的巨大困难是 1999 年开创性的“可用安全”论文“Why Johnny Can't Encrypt”（该论文或多或少发明了“可用安全”领域）的主题，该论文中提出的大多数批评对于 27 年后的 PGP 实现仍然适用。

blocks.html_en: And that brings us to the second aspect of PGP, key distribution. We've seen, at least in sketch form, how PGP email is conveyed, the first challenge of encrypted email. But what of the second challenge—is PGP for use with a directory system? Well, the earliest examples of PGP de facto were, because they did not address key distribution at all. Very early on, though, PGP gained a novel concept called a "web of trust." The web of trust, or WOT, is simultaneously one of computer security's most intriguing and appealing concepts and one of its most inane and stupid. I'll try to explain it succinctly.

sentences.en: And that brings us to the second aspect of PGP, key distribution.
sentences.zh: 这就引出了 PGP 的第二个方面，即密钥分发。

sentences.en: We've seen, at least in sketch form, how PGP email is conveyed, the first challenge of encrypted email.
sentences.zh: 我们至少以草图的形式看到了 PGP 电子邮件的传输方式，这是加密电子邮件的第一个挑战。

sentences.en: But what of the second challenge—is PGP for use with a directory system?
sentences.zh: 但是第二个挑战是什么——PGP 是否可以与目录系统一起使用？

sentences.en: Well, the earliest examples of PGP de facto were, because they did not address key distribution at all.
sentences.zh: 事实上，PGP 最早的例子是，因为它们根本没有解决密钥分发问题。

sentences.en: Very early on, though, PGP gained a novel concept called a "web of trust."
sentences.zh: 不过，PGP 很早就获得了一个称为“信任网络”的新颖概念。

sentences.en: The web of trust, or WOT, is simultaneously one of computer security's most intriguing and appealing concepts and one of its most inane and stupid.
sentences.zh: 信任网（WOT）既是计算机安全中最有趣和最吸引人的概念之一，也是最愚蠢和愚蠢的概念之一。

sentences.en: I'll try to explain it succinctly.
sentences.zh: 我将尝试简洁地解释它。

blocks.html_en: You are Alice, and you want to send an email to Bob. The "why" is not important, these are timeless characters of cryptographic explanations and they have a timeless need to securely exchange messages. If you know Bob in person, say because you are colleagues at an institution, the key distribution problem is simple. You can perform calculations on a key, like a checksum, to produce a "fingerprint" which is unique to the key and short enough to not be completely impractical to read by eye. You, Alice, walk over to Bob's office and ask to see the fingerprint of his key. You write that down on a sticky note, and then in the future, you make sure to encrypt your emails to Bob using a public key that has that correct fingerprint. Easy as pie so far, but that doesn't scale well to situations where Bob is not within walking distance.

sentences.en: You are Alice, and you want to send an email to Bob.
sentences.zh: 您是 Alice，您想向 Bob 发送一封电子邮件。

sentences.en: The "why" is not important, these are timeless characters of cryptographic explanations and they have a timeless need to securely exchange messages.
sentences.zh: “为什么”并不重要，这些是密码解释的永恒特征，并且它们具有安全交换消息的永恒需求。

sentences.en: If you know Bob in person, say because you are colleagues at an institution, the key distribution problem is simple.
sentences.zh: 如果您亲自认识鲍勃，比如说因为您是某个机构的同事，那么密钥分配问题就很简单。

sentences.en: You can perform calculations on a key, like a checksum, to produce a "fingerprint" which is unique to the key and short enough to not be completely impractical to read by eye.
sentences.zh: 您可以对密钥执行计算（例如校验和），以生成密钥独有的“指纹”，并且该指纹足够短，以至于肉眼无法完全读取。

sentences.en: You, Alice, walk over to Bob's office and ask to see the fingerprint of his key.
sentences.zh: 你，爱丽丝，走到鲍勃的办公室并要求查看他钥匙的指纹。

sentences.en: You write that down on a sticky note, and then in the future, you make sure to encrypt your emails to Bob using a public key that has that correct fingerprint.
sentences.zh: 您将其写在便利贴上，然后在将来，您确保使用具有正确指纹的公钥加密发送给 Bob 的电子邮件。

sentences.en: Easy as pie so far, but that doesn't scale well to situations where Bob is not within walking distance.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，这很简单，但是对于鲍勃不在步行距离之内的情况来说，这并不能很好地扩展。

blocks.html_en: And so the "web" enters: Bob may not be conveniently accessible to Alice because, say, he works somewhere else, but Alice's coworker Charlie might have been at a conference with Bob a few months ago. If Charlie took down Bob's key fingerprint, he can give Alice a copy when he gets back. Since Alice knows Charlie and believes him to be trustworthy, this is a pretty good substitute for getting the fingerprint straight from the horse's mouth.

sentences.en: And so the "web" enters: Bob may not be conveniently accessible to Alice because, say, he works somewhere else, but Alice's coworker Charlie might have been at a conference with Bob a few months ago.
sentences.zh: 因此，“网络”进入了：爱丽丝可能无法方便地访问鲍勃，因为他在其他地方工作，但爱丽丝的同事查理可能在几个月前与鲍勃参加了一次会议。

sentences.en: If Charlie took down Bob's key fingerprint, he can give Alice a copy when he gets back.
sentences.zh: 如果查理记下了鲍勃的关键指纹，他回来后就可以给爱丽丝一份副本。

sentences.en: Since Alice knows Charlie and believes him to be trustworthy, this is a pretty good substitute for getting the fingerprint straight from the horse's mouth.
sentences.zh: 由于爱丽丝认识查理并相信他是值得信赖的，因此这是直接从马嘴里获取指纹的一个很好的替代方案。

blocks.html_en: PGP's WOT model encodes this sneakernet approach to key distribution into a software implementation. A PGP user can "sign" another user's key, a way of cryptographically attesting that they believe that to be the correct key. These signatures are uploaded to public "keyservers" that allow anyone to query for a key and for the set of signatures it has received. If there are enough keys with enough signatures, you can treat the whole thing as a graph and try to find "trust paths" between Alice and Bob, even if doing so requires six degrees of separation1. The tangled interconnection of keys and signatures verifying them forms a web: the web of trust.

sentences.en: PGP's WOT model encodes this sneakernet approach to key distribution into a software implementation.
sentences.zh: PGP 的 WOT 模型将这种密钥分发的运动鞋网络方法编码到软件实现中。

sentences.en: A PGP user can "sign" another user's key, a way of cryptographically attesting that they believe that to be the correct key.
sentences.zh: PGP 用户可以“签署”另一个用户的密钥，这是一种以加密方式证明他们认为该密钥是正确的密钥的方法。

sentences.en: These signatures are uploaded to public "keyservers" that allow anyone to query for a key and for the set of signatures it has received.
sentences.zh: 这些签名被上传到公共“密钥服务器”，允许任何人查询密钥及其收到的签名集。

sentences.en: If there are enough keys with enough signatures, you can treat the whole thing as a graph and try to find "trust paths" between Alice and Bob, even if doing so requires six degrees of separation1.
sentences.zh: 如果有足够的密钥和足够的签名，您可以将整个事情视为一个图，并尝试找到 Alice 和 Bob 之间的“信任路径”，即使这样做需要六度分离1。

sentences.en: The tangled interconnection of keys and signatures verifying them forms a web: the web of trust.
sentences.zh: 密钥和验证它们的签名之间错综复杂的互连形成了一个网络：信任之网。

blocks.html_en: In practice, the tide seems to have turned against the WOT model by the 2010s, and today it has been completely abandoned. It didn't scale as well as its proponents had hoped, usability problems with the tooling discouraged participation, and both the implementation and concept were complex enough that poor user understanding undermined the benefits. Today the WOT has fundamental problems (mostly related to the open keyservers) that have made it practically unusable, and I think it is fair to call it a failed experiment. Further extending that fairness, we should note that the WOT was surely an inspiration for the vastly simplified key verification schemes that appear in most modern end-to-end encrypted messengers.

sentences.en: In practice, the tide seems to have turned against the WOT model by the 2010s, and today it has been completely abandoned.
sentences.zh: 在实践中，到了 2010 年代，潮流似乎已经转向反对 WOT 模型，如今它已被完全放弃。

sentences.en: It didn't scale as well as its proponents had hoped, usability problems with the tooling discouraged participation, and both the implementation and concept were complex enough that poor user understanding undermined the benefits.
sentences.zh: 它的扩展性不如其支持者所希望的那样，工具的可用性问题阻碍了参与，而且实现和概念都足够复杂，用户理解不佳削弱了其好处。

sentences.en: Today the WOT has fundamental problems (mostly related to the open keyservers) that have made it practically unusable, and I think it is fair to call it a failed experiment.
sentences.zh: 如今，WOT 存在根本性问题（主要与开放密钥服务器有关），导致其几乎无法使用，我认为称其为失败的实验是公平的。

sentences.en: Further extending that fairness, we should note that the WOT was surely an inspiration for the vastly simplified key verification schemes that appear in most modern end-to-end encrypted messengers.
sentences.zh: 进一步扩展这种公平性，我们应该注意到，WOT 无疑是大多数现代端到端加密信使中出现的大大简化的密钥验证方案的灵感来源。

blocks.html_en: From its messy academic origins to its transformation into an open project, PGP enjoyed a lot of prominence in academic and "cypherpunk" communities during the 1990s and 2000s, but was less common in corporate environments. The WOT model only made sense in the absence of a centrally maintained directory, and while PGP could be used with a directory it was not the path espoused by its advocates. PGP was mostly used among researchers and hobbyists, the cryptographic establishment. In the Enterprise, there was another player: S/MIME.

sentences.en: From its messy academic origins to its transformation into an open project, PGP enjoyed a lot of prominence in academic and "cypherpunk" communities during the 1990s and 2000s, but was less common in corporate environments.
sentences.zh: 从混乱的学术起源到转变为开放项目，PGP 在 20 世纪 90 年代和 2000 年代在学术和“密码朋克”社区中享有盛誉，但在企业环境中却不太常见。

sentences.en: The WOT model only made sense in the absence of a centrally maintained directory, and while PGP could be used with a directory it was not the path espoused by its advocates.
sentences.zh: WOT 模型只有在缺乏集中维护的目录的情况下才有意义，虽然 PGP 可以与目录一起使用，但这并不是其倡导者所支持的路径。

sentences.en: PGP was mostly used among researchers and hobbyists, the cryptographic establishment.
sentences.zh: PGP 主要在研究人员和爱好者、密码学机构中使用。

sentences.en: In the Enterprise, there was another player: S/MIME.

blocks.html_en: S/MIME

S/MIME originated with RSA Security, the company founded to commercialize the RSA encryption algorithm. RSA Security survives today as a subsidiary of teacher's pension funds, although its prominence in the industry has generally declined. Back in the 1990s, RSA was on the cutting edge, so 1996's introduction of an RSA solution for email encryption got quick attention from industry.

sentences.en: S/MIME originated with RSA Security, the company founded to commercialize the RSA encryption algorithm.

blocks.html_en: Over the following two years, RSA's product evolved into an IETF standard called S/MIME, for Secure MIME. S/MIME saw a series of new versions up to the present day, and is exceptionally rare in the academic and hobbyist world but fairly common in corporate and government environments, especially with Microsoft Exchange (having adopted S/MIME as its secure email option of choice after the failure of X.400 to take over inter-organization mail). Let's see how S/MIME handles the first challenge of encrypted email, encoding the message for transport.

blocks.html_en: Well, as the name suggests, MIME is involved. The simple sketch is like this: an email is prepared as a MIME document, which is then encrypted into the form of a PKCS#7 document. PKCS, the Public Key Cryptography Standards, refer to a series of open standards memos published by RSA Security. PKCS#7 describes "Cryptographic Message Syntax" or CMS, a standard container for an encrypted payload along with various metadata. In the terminology of the time, S/MIME is said to "enhance" messages with the "security services" of PKCS#7. We still use the term PKCS#7 today, mostly to refer to cryptographic certificates serialized in that format. For email, on the other hand, PKCS#7 has been superseded by an IETF standard called simply "CMS."

sentences.zh: PKCS#7 描述了“加密消息语法”或 CMS，它是加密有效负载以及各种元数据的标准容器。

sentences.zh: 另一方面，对于电子邮件，PKCS#7 已被称为“CMS”的 IETF 标准所取代。

blocks.html_en: The CMS version of the email, which remember is an encrypted MIME document plus cryptosystem-related metadata, is then encoded as PEM (functionally Base64) and made the body of a MIME email with a type of "application/pkcs7-mime" (yes, I just said that PKCS#7 is gone from this application, but naturally the name has stayed). This is pretty much equivalent to the MIME version of PGP emails, and has basically the same upsides and downsides: it's relatively elegant and avoids a lot of weird edge cases in clients that support it, but it's quite hostile to clients without S/MIME support, which will be unable to make anything of the message. It also keeps many of the general downsides of MIME, and for example the RFC has to spend some paragraphs justifying why binary data is base64d and then converted to binary and then base64d again. Not having the extra layers of base64 involved would break interoperability scenarios with non-S/MIME tools!

blocks.html_en: Let's consider the second aspect of the encrypted email problem, then: key distribution. Here, S/MIME takes a very X.400 style dodge. The original S/MIME standards documents, as far as I can tell, do not address key distribution in any way except for oblique and incidental references like "validating the certificate to a trust anchor." This seems odd except in the context of S/MIME's development by RSA Security and, as it became an IETF standard, Netscape—the birthplace of SSL. S/MIME was intended for use with the PKI, the Public Key Infrastructure, which has a nuanced and sometimes vague history but fell out of a combination of X.500 concepts and implementation work by companies including RSA Security and Netscape. In other words, S/MIME grew up alongside SSL/TLS, and uses the same key distribution architecture.

blocks.html_en: This is not exactly the same as saying that keys will be retrieved from a directory, but if you remember that the X.500 that gave us these concepts was itself supposed to be the directory, you can see that they are closely related ideas. In practice, they are often pretty much the same: a classic real-world application of S/MIME is in a Microsoft Active Directory environment, where the Domain Controller acts both as a directory (LDAP) and as a Certificate Authority, signing certificates for the users that populate that directory. This means that key retrieval from LDAP vs. certificate verification against the (AD) CA are pretty much the same thing, and you have the same assumptions around there being some sort of central authority to maintain the directory.

blocks.html_en: Today

So here we have the classical email encryption landscape: there are two major options, PGP and S/MIME, each of which is unsuitable for most users for its own special reasons. Out of this frustrating stalemate, several other phenomenon have emerged.

sentences.en: So here we have the classical email encryption landscape: there are two major options, PGP and S/MIME, each of which is unsuitable for most users for its own special reasons.
sentences.zh: 所以这里我们有经典的电子邮件加密景观：有两个主要选项，PGP 和 S/MIME，每个选项由于其自身的特殊原因都不适合大多数用户。

sentences.en: Out of this frustrating stalemate, several other phenomenon have emerged.
sentences.zh: 在这种令人沮丧的僵局中，还出现了其他几种现象。

blocks.html_en: First, we should briefly touch on "modern" email encryption, where "modern" can become a bit of a euphemism for "proprietary." There is a new set of email providers that offer some kind of built-in, managed E2E. Protonmail is the most prominent. Protonmail's dominant E2E encryption implementation is a proprietary, simplified system that relies on Protonmail as key directory and cryptographic implementation. This is not interoperable with other email providers, so I do not consider it a true form of "email encryption," but rather a proprietary encrypted messaging product offered alongside email. Protonmail does have two features for sending secure email to non-Protonmail recipients: the first is essentially a compatibility layer to PGP that allows Protonmail's encryption to get along with PGP tools. The second is a traditional compliance-driven "secure email" product where the recipient is actually sent a link to a website where they can enter a password to retrieve the message—once again, not really email but a separate product that loosely works with email.

blocks.html_en: Second, let's talk a bit about how email is really encrypted, in real reality, because there is this huge, grand-canyon-esque disconnect between the way that people discuss email encryption and what actually happens in the real world. Let's start taking that on by differentiating some terms: so far I have discussed end-to-end or E2E encryption, which is not exactly a well-defined technical term but generally means that a single encrypted payload travels unmodified and undecrypted from a human user to a human user. This can be distinguished from non-E2E encryption, where a message might be encrypted and decrypted several times, and using keys controlled by people other than the sender and recipient. We should also note the distinction between "encryption at rest," whether or not data is encrypted when stored (e.g. on disk), and "encryption in transit," or whether or not data is encrypted when sent over the network.

sentences.en: Second, let's talk a bit about how email is really encrypted, in real reality, because there is this huge, grand-canyon-esque disconnect between the way that people discuss email encryption and what actually happens in the real world.
sentences.zh: 其次，我们来谈谈电子邮件在现实中是如何真正加密的，因为人们讨论电子邮件加密的方式与现实世界中实际发生的情况之间存在着巨大的、大峡谷式的脱节。

sentences.en: Let's start taking that on by differentiating some terms: so far I have discussed end-to-end or E2E encryption, which is not exactly a well-defined technical term but generally means that a single encrypted payload travels unmodified and undecrypted from a human user to a human user.
sentences.zh: 让我们开始通过区分一些术语来解决这个问题：到目前为止，我已经讨论了端到端或端到端加密，这并不完全是一个定义明确的技术术语，但通常意味着单个加密的有效负载在从人类用户到人类用户时未经修改和未解密。

sentences.en: This can be distinguished from non-E2E encryption, where a message might be encrypted and decrypted several times, and using keys controlled by people other than the sender and recipient.
sentences.zh: 这可以与非端到端加密区分开来，在非端到端加密中，消息可能被加密和解密多次，并且使用由发送者和接收者以外的人控制的密钥。

sentences.en: We should also note the distinction between "encryption at rest," whether or not data is encrypted when stored (e.g. on disk), and "encryption in transit," or whether or not data is encrypted when sent over the network.
sentences.zh: 我们还应该注意“静态加密”（数据在存储时（例如在磁盘上）时是否加密）与“传输中加密”（数据在通过网络发送时是否加密）之间的区别。

blocks.html_en: Email is exchanged between email servers using SMTP, and is exchanged between email servers and clients using IMAP 2. Both of these protocols are now widely used over encryption, either using the STARTTLS method or "real" TLS, a distinction that I will not explain right now. While I lack solid data, it seems like a safe bet that the vast majority of real-world email is encrypted in transit, because at least the mail server logs that I have access to show the vast majority of mail servers using STARTTLS to secure their transfer connections. Client configuration is a more confusing topic but everything other than IMAP over TLS is now generally regarded as a big mistake, and modern clients should aggressively steer the user towards that route.

sentences.en: Email is exchanged between email servers using SMTP, and is exchanged between email servers and clients using IMAP 2.
sentences.zh: 电子邮件在电子邮件服务器之间使用 SMTP 进行交换，在电子邮件服务器和客户端之间使用 IMAP 2 进行交换。

sentences.en: Both of these protocols are now widely used over encryption, either using the STARTTLS method or "real" TLS, a distinction that I will not explain right now.
sentences.zh: 这两种协议现在都广泛用于加密，无论是使用 STARTTLS 方法还是“真正的”TLS，我现在不会解释这种区别。

sentences.en: While I lack solid data, it seems like a safe bet that the vast majority of real-world email is encrypted in transit, because at least the mail server logs that I have access to show the vast majority of mail servers using STARTTLS to secure their transfer connections.
sentences.zh: 虽然我缺乏可靠的数据，但可以肯定的是，绝大多数现实世界的电子邮件在传输过程中都是加密的，因为至少我有权访问的邮件服务器日志显示绝大多数邮件服务器使用 STARTTLS 来保护其传输连接。

sentences.en: Client configuration is a more confusing topic but everything other than IMAP over TLS is now generally regarded as a big mistake, and modern clients should aggressively steer the user towards that route.
sentences.zh: 客户端配置是一个更令人困惑的主题，但除了基于 TLS 的 IMAP 之外的所有内容现在通常被认为是一个大错误，现代客户端应该积极引导用户采用该路线。

blocks.html_en: This is a confusing contradiction: we have had this big, long, boring discussion of how email encryption has failed to go anywhere. Then I tell you that almost all email is encrypted. What gives?

sentences.en: This is a confusing contradiction: we have had this big, long, boring discussion of how email encryption has failed to go anywhere.
sentences.zh: 这是一个令人困惑的矛盾：我们对电子邮件加密为何未能取得任何进展进行了如此大而漫长且无聊的讨论。

sentences.en: Then I tell you that almost all email is encrypted.
sentences.zh: 然后我告诉你，几乎所有电子邮件都是加密的。

sentences.en: What gives?
sentences.zh: 什么给？

blocks.html_en: To start, remember our "in transit vs. at rest, and E2E vs. not-E2E, distinctions. The fact that the network connections between email participants are generally encrypted gives us a lot of security advantages, but it does not address the full scope of requirements that PGP and S/MIME do. Your email is not secure from your email provider, or the recipient's email provider for that matter, since email is usually not encrypted at rest and the provider has all the keys anyway. It also does not provide integrity protection, or non-repudiation, or any of a number of other useful security properties.

sentences.en: To start, remember our "in transit vs. at rest, and E2E vs. not-E2E, distinctions.
sentences.zh: 首先，请记住我们的“运输中与静止状态、E2E 与非 E2E”的区别。

sentences.en: The fact that the network connections between email participants are generally encrypted gives us a lot of security advantages, but it does not address the full scope of requirements that PGP and S/MIME do.
sentences.zh: 电子邮件参与者之间的网络连接通常是加密的，这一事实为我们提供了很多安全优势，但它并没有满足 PGP 和 S/MIME 的全部要求。

sentences.en: Your email is not secure from your email provider, or the recipient's email provider for that matter, since email is usually not encrypted at rest and the provider has all the keys anyway.
sentences.zh: 对于您的电子邮件提供商或收件人的电子邮件提供商而言，您的电子邮件并不安全，因为电子邮件通常不会静态加密，并且提供商无论如何都拥有所有密钥。

sentences.en: It also does not provide integrity protection, or non-repudiation, or any of a number of other useful security properties.
sentences.zh: 它还不提供完整性保护、不可否认性或任何其他有用的安全属性。

blocks.html_en: What's even worse is the technical reality. I pointed out that one of the big criticisms of PGP is that it is simply too complicated and provides too many options. This resulted in a litany of defects in PGP implementations over the years, and no small amount of insecure user behavior due to lack of usability. In-transit email encryption has very similar problems. STARTTLS, for example, is a fundamentally opportunistic mechanism. In its original form, an on-path attacker attempting to intercept an SMTP session can simply hijack it and suppress the STARTTLS offers from the two sides, preventing them ever negotiating a switch to encryption. This simple "downgrade attack" is a pervasive problem, and it's the main reason that I say that anything other than true TLS is a mistake. Unfortunately, STARTTLS is the most widespread option available for inter-provider email transfer.

sentences.en: What's even worse is the technical reality.
sentences.zh: 更糟糕的是技术现实。

sentences.en: I pointed out that one of the big criticisms of PGP is that it is simply too complicated and provides too many options.
sentences.zh: 我指出，PGP 最大的批评之一是它太复杂并且提供了太多选择。

sentences.en: This resulted in a litany of defects in PGP implementations over the years, and no small amount of insecure user behavior due to lack of usability.
sentences.zh: 这导致多年来 PGP 实现中存在一系列缺陷，并且由于缺乏可用性而导致大量不安全的用户行为。

sentences.en: In-transit email encryption has very similar problems.
sentences.zh: 传输中的电子邮件加密也存在非常相似的问题。

sentences.en: STARTTLS, for example, is a fundamentally opportunistic mechanism.
sentences.zh: 例如，STARTTLS 本质上是一种机会主义机制。

sentences.en: In its original form, an on-path attacker attempting to intercept an SMTP session can simply hijack it and suppress the STARTTLS offers from the two sides, preventing them ever negotiating a switch to encryption.
sentences.zh: 在其原始形式中，试图拦截 SMTP 会话的沿途攻击者可以简单地劫持它并抑制来自双方的 STARTTLS 提议，从而阻止他们协商切换到加密。

sentences.en: This simple "downgrade attack" is a pervasive problem, and it's the main reason that I say that anything other than true TLS is a mistake.
sentences.zh: 这种简单的“降级攻击”是一个普遍存在的问题，这也是我说除真正的 TLS 之外的任何内容都是错误的主要原因。

sentences.en: Unfortunately, STARTTLS is the most widespread option available for inter-provider email transfer.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，STARTTLS 是可用于提供商间电子邮件传输的最广泛的选项。

blocks.html_en: That said, there are ways to address this problem. For example, DANE, the DNS-based scheme for TLS certificate distribution, has failed in the HTTP arena but has decent adoption (although very far from universal) in email transfer. It does a good job of providing mail servers with a way to discover that their peers are capable of encryption, without doing so over the SMTP channel itself. Even without DNSSEC to fully protect the DANE channel, this is an improvement that prevents many common attack scenarios. There is an even more popular approach that, while clumsy, avoids the dependence on DNSSEC to avoid a second on-path attack on the key channel: MTA-STS. MTA-STS is similar to DANE in that it provides an independent channel for a mail server to find if a peer supports TLS, but it uses HTTPS for that second channel, thus inheriting the protections of CA-rooted TLS.

sentences.en: That said, there are ways to address this problem.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，有一些方法可以解决这个问题。

sentences.en: For example, DANE, the DNS-based scheme for TLS certificate distribution, has failed in the HTTP arena but has decent adoption (although very far from universal) in email transfer.
sentences.zh: 例如，DANE（基于 DNS 的 TLS 证书分发方案）在 HTTP 领域失败了，但在电子邮件传输中得到了不错的采用（尽管远未普及）。

sentences.en: It does a good job of providing mail servers with a way to discover that their peers are capable of encryption, without doing so over the SMTP channel itself.
sentences.zh: 它很好地为邮件服务器提供了一种发现其对等方能够加密的方法，而无需通过 SMTP 通道本身进行加密。

sentences.en: Even without DNSSEC to fully protect the DANE channel, this is an improvement that prevents many common attack scenarios.
sentences.zh: 即使没有 DNSSEC 来完全保护 DANE 通道，这也是一项可以防止许多常见攻击场景的改进。

sentences.en: There is an even more popular approach that, while clumsy, avoids the dependence on DNSSEC to avoid a second on-path attack on the key channel: MTA-STS.
sentences.zh: 还有一种更流行的方法，虽然笨拙，但却避免了对 DNSSEC 的依赖，从而避免了对关键通道的第二次路径攻击：MTA-STS。

sentences.en: MTA-STS is similar to DANE in that it provides an independent channel for a mail server to find if a peer supports TLS, but it uses HTTPS for that second channel, thus inheriting the protections of CA-rooted TLS.
sentences.zh: MTA-STS 与 DANE 类似，它为邮件服务器提供一个独立的通道来查找对等方是否支持 TLS，但它使用 HTTPS 作为第二个通道，从而继承了基于 CA 的 TLS 的保护。

blocks.html_en: Both DANE and MTA-STS are ways of "tipping" a mail server that a peer has STARTTLS support, at which point the mail server knows that it should only connect via STARTTLS, even if the mail server on the actual SMTP channel claims not to support it. While the extra moving parts are unfortunate, this does a lot to close the downgrade attack window, and email transferred between mail providers with DANE or MTA-STS support is really pretty well secured, at least on par with most instant messaging solutions.

sentences.en: Both DANE and MTA-STS are ways of "tipping" a mail server that a peer has STARTTLS support, at which point the mail server knows that it should only connect via STARTTLS, even if the mail server on the actual SMTP channel claims not to support it.
sentences.zh: DANE 和 MTA-STS 都是“提示”邮件服务器对等方支持 STARTTLS 的方式，此时邮件服务器知道它应该只通过 STARTTLS 进行连接，即使实际 SMTP 通道上的邮件服务器声称不支持它。

sentences.en: While the extra moving parts are unfortunate, this does a lot to close the downgrade attack window, and email transferred between mail providers with DANE or MTA-STS support is really pretty well secured, at least on par with most instant messaging solutions.
sentences.zh: 虽然额外的移动部件很不幸，但这对于关闭降级攻击窗口有很大帮助，并且在具有 DANE 或 MTA-STS 支持的邮件提供商之间传输的电子邮件确实得到了很好的保护，至少与大多数即时消息解决方案相当。

blocks.html_en: This also implies that you can improve your email security a lot by just manually pinning major peers—e.g. configuring your mail server that, regardless of everything else, connections to major providers like gmail.com must be encrypted. This is actually a pretty common practice in some industries, including internal processes to maintain the list of "secure email destinations" so that it includes business partners and other frequent recipients.

sentences.en: This also implies that you can improve your email security a lot by just manually pinning major peers—e.g. configuring your mail server that, regardless of everything else, connections to major providers like gmail.com must be encrypted.
sentences.zh: 这也意味着您只需手动固定主要对等点即可大大提高电子邮件安全性 - 例如配置您的邮件服务器，无论其他一切如何，与 gmail.com 等主要提供商的连接都必须加密。

sentences.en: This is actually a pretty common practice in some industries, including internal processes to maintain the list of "secure email destinations" so that it includes business partners and other frequent recipients.
sentences.zh: 这实际上是某些行业中相当常见的做法，包括维护“安全电子邮件目的地”列表的内部流程，以便其中包括业务合作伙伴和其他频繁收件人。

blocks.html_en: In some industries, this is taken to an extreme: within the healthcare sector, for example, there are healthcare information exchanges that operate secure messaging platforms that are mostly just conventional email servers configured to refuse any non-TLS SMTP exchanges. This is sufficient to meet NIST 800-43 recommendations which is sufficient to meet the NIST security architecture for healthcare information. That might be surprising to you but it illustrates how this topic gets confusing: we all know that "email is not secure," but that's really a configuration problem with the "public" email system rather than a technical limitation. Few compliance standards actually require E2E, and securing email in transit and at rest, once you drop the E2E requirement, is so easy that it sometimes happens just by default when SMTP servers have STARTTLS support and disk encryption.

sentences.en: In some industries, this is taken to an extreme: within the healthcare sector, for example, there are healthcare information exchanges that operate secure messaging platforms that are mostly just conventional email servers configured to refuse any non-TLS SMTP exchanges.
sentences.zh: 在某些行业中，这种情况被推向极端：例如，在医疗保健领域，存在运行安全消息平台的医疗保健信息交换，这些安全消息平台大多只是配置为拒绝任何非 TLS SMTP 交换的传统电子邮件服务器。

sentences.en: This is sufficient to meet NIST 800-43 recommendations which is sufficient to meet the NIST security architecture for healthcare information.
sentences.zh: 这足以满足 NIST 800-43 建议，也足以满足医疗信息的 NIST 安全架构。

sentences.en: That might be surprising to you but it illustrates how this topic gets confusing: we all know that "email is not secure," but that's really a configuration problem with the "public" email system rather than a technical limitation.
sentences.zh: 这可能会让您感到惊讶，但它说明了这个主题如何变得令人困惑：我们都知道“电子邮件不安全”，但这实际上是“公共”电子邮件系统的配置问题，而不是技术限制。

sentences.en: Few compliance standards actually require E2E, and securing email in transit and at rest, once you drop the E2E requirement, is so easy that it sometimes happens just by default when SMTP servers have STARTTLS support and disk encryption.
sentences.zh: 很少有合规性标准实际上需要 E2E，一旦放弃 E2E 要求，保护传输中和静态电子邮件的安全就非常容易，以至于有时当 SMTP 服务器具有 STARTTLS 支持和磁盘加密时，默认情况下就会发生这种情况。

blocks.html_en: If you look at healthcare marketing materials, you'll sometimes see STARTTLS and S/MIME described as alternatives to each other. This doesn't make much technical sense, but you have to think about the context. If you have to communicate over mail servers whose configuration you do not control (or at least have insight into), you don't have a way of ensuring that your messages will be encrypted in transit unless you use E2E encryption such as S/MIME. If you are sending mail between servers that you do control, though, you can ensure encryption in transit by configuring the mail servers that way, and you do not need E2E encryption to meet that specific aim. Taken in this view, E2E encryption is almost a workaround for a defect of the design of email, and if you have taken anything from this longer than expected journey I hope it will be that: the history of encrypted email is so odd, and full of so many false starts, because it is basically the history of E2E encryption as an overlay "fix" for email's lack of the level of security provided by newer messaging solutions out of the box.

sentences.zh: 从这个角度来看，E2E 加密几乎是电子邮件设计缺陷的一种解决方法，如果您从这个比预期更长的旅程中获得了任何东西，我希望它会是：加密电子邮件的历史是如此奇怪，并且充满了如此多的错误开始，因为它基本上是 E2E 加密的历史，作为电子邮件缺乏新的消息传递解决方案提供的安全级别的覆盖“修复”。

blocks.html_en: This also helps to explain the lack of adoption: it's not really that we can't figure out how to encrypt email, we do encrypt email. But as with most communications technology, the "security layer" has been pushed onto the service providers rather than the users. This has significant downsides, since it requires trust in those service providers, but one huge, conspicuous upside: if encryption is transparent to the user, people will actually use it.

blocks.html_en: • The idea that all humans are separated by six degrees at most was popularized in 1990 and undoubtedly had influence on the PGP WOT design, as well as a number of other similar connection-following social technology ideas of the same period. As it turns out, the whole idea came from fiction and has little grounding in actual statistical research on human social networks, which is at least suggestive of a reason why none of these web models have been successful.↩
